name: MarketSurveillanceReflex
description: Data Activator Reflex triggers for real-time market manipulation detection. Replaces ACA polling (10s latency) with sub-second KQL-driven triggers for each manipulation rule.

triggers.name: Spoofing Alert Trigger
triggers.description: Fires when a broker exhibits >80% cancel rate with <500ms latency on large orders within a 60-second window.
triggers.kql_query:
ORDER_BOOK_EVENTS
| where event_time > ago(1m)
| summarize
    total_adds    = countif(action == 'add'),
    total_cancels = countif(action == 'cancel'),
    avg_qty       = avg(iff(action == 'add', toreal(quantity), real(null))),
    avg_cancel_ms = avg(iff(action == 'cancel', toreal(datetime_diff('millisecond', event_time, prev(event_time))), real(null)))
    by exchange_id, symbol, broker_id, bin(event_time, 1m)
| where total_adds > 0
| extend cancel_rate = todouble(total_cancels) / todouble(total_adds)
| where cancel_rate >= 0.80
    and avg_qty >= 10000
    and avg_cancel_ms <= 500

actions.name: TeamsAlert
actions.message_template: 🚨 **SPOOFING DETECTED** | {{exchange_id}} / {{symbol}} | Broker: {{broker_id}} | Cancel rate: {{cancel_rate | percent}} | Avg cancel: {{avg_cancel_ms}}ms

triggers.name: Layering Alert Trigger
triggers.description: Fires when a broker places orders at ≥5 distinct price levels with ≥70% cancellation fraction plus opposite-side orders within 2 minutes.
triggers.kql_query:
ORDER_BOOK_EVENTS
| where event_time > ago(2m)
| summarize
    price_levels  = dcount(price),
    total_adds    = countif(action == 'add'),
    total_cancels = countif(action == 'cancel')
    by exchange_id, symbol, broker_id, side, bin(event_time, 2m)
| where total_adds > 0
| extend cancel_fraction = todouble(total_cancels) / todouble(total_adds)
| where price_levels >= 5 and cancel_fraction >= 0.70

actions.name: TeamsAlert
actions.message_template: 🚨 **LAYERING DETECTED** | {{exchange_id}} / {{symbol}} | Broker: {{broker_id}} | {{price_levels}} price levels | Cancel: {{cancel_fraction | percent}}

triggers.name: Wash Trading Alert Trigger
triggers.description: Fires when ≥3 trades are detected between accounts sharing the same ultimate beneficial owner within a 10-minute window.
triggers.kql_query:
TRADES
| where event_time > ago(10m)
| where buyer_id != seller_id
| join kind=inner (
    BROKER_OWNERSHIP
    | project account_id, ultimate_owner_buyer = ultimate_owner
) on $left.buyer_id == $right.account_id
| join kind=inner (
    BROKER_OWNERSHIP
    | project account_id, ultimate_owner_seller = ultimate_owner
) on $left.seller_id == $right.account_id
| where ultimate_owner_buyer == ultimate_owner_seller
| summarize
    wash_count  = count(),
    wash_volume = sum(quantity),
    account_a   = any(buyer_id),
    account_b   = any(seller_id)
    by symbol, exchange_id, ultimate_owner = ultimate_owner_buyer, bin(event_time, 10m)
| where wash_count >= 3

triggers.name: Volume Anomaly Alert Trigger
triggers.description: Fires when 1-minute trade volume exceeds the rolling 60-minute mean by ≥3 standard deviations (Z-score ≥ 3.0).
triggers.kql_query:
let volume_series =
    TRADES
    | where event_time > ago(1h)
    | summarize volume_1m = sum(quantity)
        by symbol, exchange_id, bin(event_time, 1m);
volume_series
| join kind=inner (
    volume_series
    | summarize
        mean_volume_1h = avg(volume_1m),
        std_volume_1h  = stdev(volume_1m)
        by symbol, exchange_id
) on symbol, exchange_id
| extend z_score = iff(
    std_volume_1h > 0,
    (volume_1m - mean_volume_1h) / std_volume_1h,
    0.0
)
| where z_score >= 3.0
| where event_time > ago(2m)

environment_variables.INTERVENTION_AGENT_WEBHOOK_URL: HTTP webhook URL of the Intervention Agent API (deployed on ACA or Azure Functions)
environment_variables.TEAMS_WEBHOOK_URL: Microsoft Teams incoming webhook URL for human-readable alerts

deployment_notes: 1. Create a Data Activator Reflex in the Fabric workspace via the Fabric portal.
2. Connect the Reflex to the Eventhouse 'surveillance' database as the data source.
3. Import this file or paste each trigger KQL query into the Reflex editor.
4. Set environment variable values (KQL_URI, webhook URLs) in the Reflex secrets store.
5. Enable each trigger and verify sub-second detection in the Reflex monitoring view.
6. The webhook action calls the Intervention Agent API which opens an InterventionCase and notifies the regulator.
7. The Teams notification provides a human-readable alert to the surveillance desk.
8. For detailed step-by-step portal instructions, see docs/data-activator-setup.md.
